nodes.title: Validate the target before binding
nodes.why: `bind` only makes sense for callable values, so this gives a clear error early.
nodes.alternative: Skip the check and let `.apply` fail later.
nodes.tradeoff: More predictable error message with a tiny upfront check.

nodes.title: Detect wrapper construction with new.target
nodes.why: new.target distinguishes `new boundFn()` from an ordinary call even when a caller supplies an object inheriting from boundFn.prototype.
nodes.alternative: Check `this instanceof boundFn`, which produces a false constructor classification for such ordinary receivers.
nodes.tradeoff: Reliable detection for the documented classic-constructor subset without claiming full native bound-function semantics.

nodes.title: Preserve prototype chain for instanceof
nodes.why: For a classic constructor with an object prototype, this keeps instances from `new boundFn()` linked to the original function’s prototype.
nodes.tradeoff: Useful for the scoped exercise without reproducing native bind's exotic prototype and Symbol.hasInstance behavior.
